channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: Trump pledges more Iran attacks, saying U.S. will be 'attacking them very hard'
item.description: President Donald Trump promised more attacks on Iran after strikes resumed this week. Earlier this week, he said a deal to end the war was near.

item.title: Here's the inflation breakdown for May 2026 — in one chart
item.description: Inflation jumped to a three-year high in May as the Iran war continued to fuel higher energy prices.

item.title: Amazon trucking expansion sparks freight stock sell-off
item.description: Amazon has been spinning out more of its in-house logistics offerings for others to access, posing a growing threat to industry incumbents.

item.title: Trump signs $70 billion immigration funding bill after months of delay
item.description: The $70 billion package will fund Immigration and Customs Enforcement and Customs and Border Protection through the end of President Donald Trump's term.

item.title: Bill Gates tells House panel 'I should have never met' with Jeffrey Epstein
item.description: Gates reportedly prepared for his testimony with the help of Jake Greenberg, who previously was the Oversight committee's chief investigations counsel.

item.title: An option trade to play the volatility in oil prices as Middle East tensions persist
item.description: Tony Zhang looks at a way to monetize elevated energy price volatility.

item.title: Trump doubles down on Pulte for intelligence post, calls for short-term extension of foreign surveillance law
item.description: Unless Congress acts, a key provision of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act will expire at the end of this week.

item.title: SpaceX primed for double-digit pop on first day, if 'perps' trading is any guide
item.description: Crypto traders are currently pricing the SpaceX pre-IPO perpetual future on Hyperliquid at $162, about 20% above the company's fixed IPO share price of $135.

item.title: Palantir's Karp says businesses are 'unhappy' with the frontier AI labs
item.description: Palantir CEO Alex Karp says AI will drive the most important political decisions in the U.S. and shouldn't be decided by party lines.

item.title: Trump says Iran will 'pay the price' and claims they have 'taken too long' to agree to a deal
item.description: The president's comments came after U.S. forces on Tuesday evening launched strikes against Iran.

item.title: College sticker prices top $100,000 at 16 schools — but many students pay significantly less
item.description: The cost of attendance at a growing number of colleges is now more than six figures a year, according to data from The Princeton Review.

item.title: Beef prices finally fell, but cattle herd screwworm could eat into consumers' inflation relief
item.description: The arrival of the invasive New World screwworm in Texas could hit consumers this summer with beef prices already an acute source of inflation.

item.title: Regulators' proposed prediction markets rules ban trading on terrorism, assassinations
item.description: The proposed rules from the commission will now face a public comment period.

item.title: Private credit lenders say their big software bet faces an AI reckoning — but not a ‘SaaSpocalypse'
item.description: Private credit's exposure to software is being tested by AI, as lenders look past “SaaSpocalypse” fears to identify winners and losers.

item.title: Autonomous freight trucking company Einride rises sharply in first trade on Nasdaq
item.description: Self-driving freight truck technology company Einride rose significant in its first day of trading on the Nasdaq after a SPAC deal.

item.title: North Carolina treasurer passes on SpaceX citing valuation concerns; favors OpenAI, Anthropic
item.description: North Carolina has invested heavily in AI startups OpenAI and Anthropic while avoiding a direct stake in SpaceX.

item.title: Investors are fleeing tech stocks in record numbers
item.description: Bank of America clients sold a record $14.2 billion in single stocks last week

item.title: The World Cup will likely be the biggest gambling event in history
item.description: Global wagers on the 2026 World Cup could top $50 billion, according to Macquarie analyst Chad Beynon. That’s up from more than $35 billion in the 2022 tournament.

item.title: Weekly mortgage demand surges nearly 11% higher, despite volatile interest rates
item.description: Mortgage demand from both current homeowners and potential buyers rose sharply, even though interest rates climbed as well.

item.title: Two reasons for optimism after Tuesday's whipsaw market sell-off
item.description: There's reason for optimism, judging by bullish options flows around stocks that do better when interest-rates stay lower, and call-buyers who are prepared for the biggest move in Oracle since Covid.

item.title: We're paring another stock position to bring our cash weighting closer to 12%
item.description: This stock hasn't been a win, but it has quietly risen 10% since our last purchase.

item.title: Want more travel rewards? Start with your bank account
item.description: Mileage savings accounts and co-branded airline/hotel debit cards help you earn travel rewards without having to take on credit.

item.title: How pandemic car shortages are still making new and used cars expensive
item.description: Pandemic-era shortages are still pushing up used car prices, and automakers learned lessons from that time that stands to keep supply tight

item.title: Gold, silver and bitcoin fall as traders up Fed rate hike bets
item.description: Precious metals were firmly in negative territory, as fears about inflation and the Federal Reserve's interest rate path weighed on investor sentiment.

item.title: SpaceX IPO explained: The price is set, but retail allocation still up in the air
item.description: Charles Schwab, Fidelity, Robinhood, SoFi and Morgan Stanley's E-Trade are among the brokerage platforms making SpaceX shares available.

item.title: Novelis restarts production at New York plant supplying Ford
item.description: Novelis said on Wednesday it restarted production at its New York facility, a plant key to Ford's pickup truck line, months after two fires halted operations.

item.title: $70 billion immigration funding package passes in U.S. House in a win for Speaker Johnson
item.description: The U.S. House on Tuesday approved a $70 billion immigration enforcement funding package after months of debate.

item.title: GM eyes new battery chemistry to grow AI data center, energy storage business
item.description: GM is expanding efforts to capitalize on the expected growth of energy storage and data centers and the development of next-generation sodium-ion batteries.

item.title: Super Micro stock falls 13% on $7 billion financing plans as company touts AI server orders
item.description: Super Micro is the latest company tied to the AI boom to announce that it's tapping the capital markets.

item.title: AI will help young workers 'mature' faster by automating grunt work, Thoma Bravo says, amid youth job crisis
item.description: "If you look at our associates, they're spending a lot less time doing models or comparables than before," Orlando Bravo, founder of Thoma Bravo, told CNBC.
